description: Introduce explicit reference-derived write metadata in the relational write-plan contracts for both propagated reference identity column bindings and key-unification member inventory. Acceptance: propagated reference identity bindings and unified reference-derived members are no longer represented as ordinary JSON scalar reads; the external contract, DTO, codec, and manifest-summary layers can identify each derived binding or member by reference site plus logical reference-member path; later key-unification work can consume the new contract shape without reopening serialization changes; the design continues to use existing `ResolvedReferenceSet` and `DocumentReference.DocumentIdentity` rather than widening the resolver payload; and compiler, codec, or runtime mismatches in the new metadata shape fail fast as internal invariant breaches rather than degrading to generic scalar metadata.
steps-to-verify: Add or update `NormalizedPlanContractCodec` and `MappingSetManifestJsonEmitter` coverage so reference-derived column bindings and key-unification members survive round-tripping, appear in normalized and manifest output, and invalid DTO or manifest mutations fail fast instead of silently collapsing back to generic scalar shape.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Update `WritePlanCompiler` and related helpers to compile non-unified propagated reference identity bindings from explicit reference-derived sources, plus any shared source-kind plumbing that flattening and later unified-member planning require. Acceptance: non-unified propagated scalar identity columns compile from reference-derived sources instead of `WriteValueSource.Scalar`; descriptor-backed propagated identity bindings compile from descriptor-document-id storage rather than raw URI strings; the compiler emits the shared provenance or source metadata needed downstream without taking ownership of key-unified member precedence; and any narrow model or DDL cleanup stays limited to what this compilation work requires.
steps-to-verify: Extend `Given_WritePlanCompiler_BindingsAndSources` coverage for direct propagated reference identity columns and descriptor-backed reference identity members.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Given_WritePlanCompiler_BindingsAndSources"`

description: Extend `KeyUnificationWritePlanCompiler` so key-unified reference identity sites use reference-derived producers instead of the old JSON-bound scalar path. Acceptance: canonical storage columns for unified reference identity parts are computed from explicit reference-derived members; mixed classes can include both ordinary JSON-bound members and reference-derived members without contract churn; reference-derived members take deterministic precedence when both can feed the same canonical storage column; descriptor-backed unified members resolve to descriptor `DocumentId` values; optional or absent reference sites keep presence-gated aliases semantically absent; and reference-derived provenance remains discoverable by reference site plus member path for follow-on profile accounting in DMS-1124.
steps-to-verify: Add or update `Given_WritePlanCompiler_KeyUnification` tests for reference-derived canonical producers, one mixed-source case proving reference-derived precedence over an ordinary JSON-bound member for the same canonical column, descriptor-backed members, and one optional absent-site case.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Given_WritePlanCompiler_KeyUnification"`

description: Extend the flattening-time resolved-reference lookup adapter to expose propagated identity-part values without changing the external resolver contract. Acceptance: the compiled reference-derived binding order is the authoritative bridge between local propagated bindings and ordered `DocumentReference.DocumentIdentity` values for the matched resolved reference occurrence; no path-matching implementation is introduced between reference-object member paths and target-identity paths; scalar identity parts are sourced from ordered `DocumentReference.DocumentIdentity` values; descriptor-backed identity parts are sourced from existing descriptor-resolution results and materialize local descriptor `DocumentId` values; nested collection occurrences resolve by ordinal path; and count or order drift between compiled metadata and resolved reference payloads fails deterministically instead of silently falling back to request JSON.
steps-to-verify: Add focused backend unit coverage for root and nested reference lookups, descriptor-backed propagated members, and count or order invariant-breach cases.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Update `RelationalWriteFlattener` to materialize propagated reference identity columns and unified canonical storage columns from resolved reference-derived values on both create and existing-document flows. Acceptance: root and nested reference sites populate `..._DocumentId` plus propagated identity storage from resolved reference data rather than raw body scalars; descriptor-backed and key-unified reference identity members materialize the correct canonical or storage columns and presence flags; one explicit optional-absent-path case proves aliases remain semantically absent while canonical storage stays the real propagation target; and impossible reference-derived mismatches fail fast instead of degrading to generic scalar reads.
steps-to-verify: Extend `Given_RelationalWriteFlattener` tests for direct propagated bindings, unified reference-derived members, descriptor-backed reference members, and optional absent-site behavior.

description: Add focused PostgreSQL integration proof for the core DMS-985 runtime population path. description: Close the gap between Core reference extraction and backend flattening so propagated-reference write requests enforce the story's all-or-none semantics with deterministic request-validation failures instead of internal invariant exceptions. Acceptance: an empty reference object and any partially populated propagated reference object are both rejected as `Data Validation Failed` with concrete JSON-path keyed validation errors; Core no longer treats a present-but-empty reference object as if the optional reference site were absent; backend flattening no longer throws `InvalidOperationException` when a reference object is present but the resolved lookup set has no matching occurrence; missing reference-derived values and missing document-reference lookups surface as `WriteValidationFailure` entries rather than uncaught internal errors; and at least one POST plus one PUT scenario prove the final response shape is stable for root and nested reference sites.
steps-to-verify: Add Core coverage around `ReferenceExtractor` or the write-validation middleware path for an empty reference object and a partially populated reference object, asserting concrete path-specific validation failures instead of silent omission or raw `InvalidOperationException` behavior.

Add backend unit coverage in `RelationalWriteFlattenerTests` or executor-level tests for present reference objects whose lookup entries are missing, asserting `RelationalWriteRequestValidationException` and path-specific `WriteValidationFailure` output for direct document-reference bindings and reference-derived members.

description: Harden propagated-reference identity extraction so malformed present members fail as deterministic write-validation errors instead of escaping as internal `500` exceptions. Acceptance: when a propagated reference identity member is present but `null`, a JSON object, or a JSON array, Core returns `400 Data Validation Failed` with a concrete JSON-path keyed validation error instead of allowing `InvalidOperationException` from `SelectNodesAndLocationFromArrayPathCoerceToStrings()` or `ReferenceExtractor` to escape; the behavior is consistent for root and nested reference sites; malformed present members are reported as invalid-present values rather than silently treated as missing optional members; empty reference objects and partial reference objects continue to report all-or-none failures deterministically; and middleware only maps request-shape validation failures, leaving true invariant breaches as internal errors.

Add focused unit coverage in `ReferenceExtractorTests` for root and nested propagated reference identity members with `null`, `{}`, and `[]` values, asserting `ReferenceExtractionValidationException` with deterministic path-specific failures.

Add middleware coverage in `ExtractDocumentInfoMiddlewareTests` proving POST and PUT requests with malformed propagated reference identity members return `400` validation responses instead of uncaught `InvalidOperationException` behavior.

description: Replace the current order-only bridge between Core-extracted `DocumentIdentity` elements and compiled propagated-reference bindings with explicit target-identity metadata. Acceptance: `DocumentReferenceBinding.IdentityBindings[*]` carries both the logical request-member path (`ReferenceJsonPath`) and the authoritative target identity path (`IdentityJsonPath`) taken from ApiSchema `referenceJsonPaths[]`; `ReferenceBindingPass`, external relational-model contracts, manifest emission, normalized-plan codecs, and any write-plan fixtures preserve both paths without reordering them; same-site duplicate `ReferenceJsonPath` groups remain supported because the compiled binding now retains the distinct target identity path for each upstream identity binding; and malformed or stale compiled metadata that omits or mismatches `IdentityJsonPath` fails fast during compilation or contract decoding instead of leaving runtime materialization to infer intent from ordinal position alone.
steps-to-verify: Extend the relational-model and plan-contract types so `ReferenceIdentityBinding` round-trips both `identity_json_path` and `reference_json_path`, then update manifest and normalized-plan golden or round-trip coverage to assert both fields are preserved in-order for ordinary bindings and same-site duplicate logical-member groups.

Add or update compiler tests proving duplicate `ReferenceJsonPath` bindings on one reference site remain legal only when they retain distinct `IdentityJsonPath` values from ApiSchema, and that malformed contract or DTO mutations involving missing or inconsistent `IdentityJsonPath` fail deterministically.

description: Make propagated-reference runtime materialization resolve the expected identity element by compiled target identity path rather than trusting positional alignment alone. Acceptance: `FlatteningResolvedReferenceLookupSet` resolves `column -> compiled identity binding -> expected IdentityJsonPath -> resolved DocumentIdentityElement`, while still using the compiled column or binding identity to support same-site duplicate `ReferenceJsonPath` groups; scalar-vs-scalar or descriptor-vs-descriptor permutations that previously could swap values silently now fail deterministically with diagnostics naming the expected and actual identity paths; descriptor-backed members continue to work for both synthetic descriptor identity paths and target-identity-path variants, but only when they correspond to the compiled binding's expected identity path; and `RelationalWriteFlattener` can no longer populate the wrong propagated column solely because two same-kind identity elements arrived in a different order.
steps-to-verify: Refactor `FlatteningResolvedReferenceLookupSet` so binding-column resolution yields the full compiled `ReferenceIdentityBinding`, then use its `IdentityJsonPath` to select or validate the resolved identity element instead of indexing directly into `DocumentIdentityElements` by ordinal alone.

Add focused unit coverage for same-kind order drift, missing expected identity paths, duplicate logical-member groups with distinct `IdentityJsonPath` values, and descriptor-backed propagated members so silent value swaps are replaced with deterministic invariant failures or correct materialization.
Add or update `RelationalWriteFlattenerTests` so a misordered resolved identity payload cannot silently populate persisted propagated columns on create or existing-document flows.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~FlatteningResolvedReferenceLookupSet|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalWriteFlattener"`

category: Cross-Layer Reference Identity Drift Regression Net
description: Add explicit regression coverage for the fragile Core-to-backend reference identity ordering contract so future refactors cannot reintroduce silent propagated-column corruption. Acceptance: there is at least one paired Core plus backend test case, or one seam-level fixture, that uses a reference site with multiple same-kind identity members whose `referenceJsonPaths[]` order is intentionally permuted to prove the system either resolves by `IdentityJsonPath` or fails deterministically rather than swapping values; another case proves supported same-site duplicate `ReferenceJsonPath` groups still materialize all intended propagated columns after the hardening work; and the tests document the true contract clearly enough that future changes to ApiSchema extraction, mapping-set compilation, or serialized plan codecs cannot accidentally collapse back to the old order-only assumption without breaking coverage.
steps-to-verify: Add Core extraction coverage that locks in the authoritative `referenceJsonPaths[]` order and preserved `IdentityJsonPath` values for multi-part references and same-site duplicate logical-member groups.

Add backend seam or runtime tests that build a resolved reference payload with the same logical members in a different same-kind order and assert deterministic failure or correct `IdentityJsonPath`-based materialization rather than silent column swaps.
Document the superseded assumption from the original DMS-985 reference-lookup work in test names or comments so future maintainers understand why positional matching alone is unsafe.

description: Refactor the relational descriptor extraction seam so Core reads descriptor-edge metadata from the mapping set's concrete resource model instead of forcing a compiled write-plan lookup inside `ExtractDocumentInfoMiddleware`. Acceptance: `DescriptorExtractor.ExtractRelationalDescriptors` no longer calls `GetWritePlanOrThrow` for non-descriptor resources; the seam still extracts descriptor-valued members under `documentPathsMapping.referenceJsonPaths` with concrete indexed JSON paths and correct descriptor resource typing; descriptor resources continue to use the shared descriptor path without requiring relational write-plan metadata; and missing or intentionally unsupported write plans remain backend-owned guard rails rather than middleware-owned failures.
steps-to-verify: Add or extend a mapping-set lookup helper that exposes the concrete relational resource model or `DescriptorEdgeSources` without requiring `WritePlansByResource` to contain a compiled write plan entry.
Refactor `DescriptorExtractor` and `ExtractDocumentInfoMiddleware` to use that model-level metadata for relational descriptor extraction.

Add or update Core unit tests proving relational descriptor extraction still covers descriptor-valued members under `referenceJsonPaths`, including at least one collection-backed path with concrete indices.

description: Restore the intended failure boundary for missing or unsupported relational write plans so they are classified by the repository and handlers instead of surfacing as middleware exceptions. Acceptance: a non-descriptor relational POST or PUT whose mapping set lacks a compiled write plan reaches `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.ExecuteWriteGuardRails`; the repository continues to return the deterministic `UnknownFailure` message for missing or unsupported write plans; `ExtractDocumentInfoMiddleware` no longer turns this state into an unhandled exception; and writable-profile requests remain aligned with `ProfileWritePipelineMiddleware` by treating missing plan states as guarded skip behavior before repository-owned classification.
steps-to-verify: Add regression coverage around the relational write request path proving a missing write plan no longer fails inside `ExtractDocumentInfoMiddleware` and instead surfaces through the repository-owned failure classification.
Add or update handler or frontend-path assertions so the final response body and status code continue to match the existing deterministic `UnknownFailure` path for missing write plans.
